sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section VI, List of Answers on Topics 75:1

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section VI, List of Answers on Topics 75:1
items.html: Three lights: 1. The light that came out through the Einayim, from which comes the primary self of the Nekudim, although it is the phase of Katnut of Nekudim. 2. The light that is extended through the coupling of AB SAG, breached the Parsa, and shone to the Nekudim through Yesod of AK. From here comes the Gadlut of Nekudim. It is considered a mere addition; it is not considered the light of Nekudim itself. 3. That which is extended by the looking of the Einayim in AHP, which is only for the purpose of the vessels of Nekudim. (Items 17, 18, 19)
